displayTitle: The IQI: Proof Your Technique Worked

beats.narration: You can't calibrate a weld. So code radiographs carry an image quality indicator — hole-type or wire — proving the technique worked.

payload.title: PROOF ON FILM
payload.sub: The small device that proves the technique worked.

beats.narration: The hole type: a thin plaque, two percent of part thickness, holes at one, two, and four times its thickness, and lead identity numbers.
The standard level reads two dash two T: plaque two percent of thickness, two T hole clearly visible. Wires: one named essential wire.
Place it source side, where quality is hardest. If that's blocked, film side with a lead F. On welds, shim the plaque.

chips.t: Shim under the plaque on welds

beats.narration: A drilled hole is an easy target. The I Q I proves the technique, not the flaw. Finding the flaw is your job.

payload.sub: Finding the flaw is your job.
